questions.question: Explain the difference between setjmp/longjmp and sigsetjmp/siglongjmp.
keyPoints: setjmp/longjmp cannot save/restore signal masks
sigsetjmp/siglongjmp can optionally save and restore signal masks via savemask parameter
sigsetjmp uses sigjmp_buf instead of jmp_buf
This difference is crucial for proper signal handling in UNIX/LINUX systems

questions.question: What is the purpose of the savemask parameter in sigsetjmp? Explain with example values.
keyPoints: savemask is an integer parameter (0 or non-zero)
If savemask = 0: signal mask is NOT saved, behaves like setjmp
If savemask ≠ 0: current signal mask is saved into the jump buffer
When siglongjmp is called, it restores the saved mask if savemask was non-zero

questions.question: Write the function prototype for sigsetjmp and sigint siglongjmp.
keyPoints: int sigsetjmp(sigjmp_buf env, int savemask)
void siglongjmp(sigjmp_buf env, int val)
sigjmp_buf is a structure for storing context
Both functions are declared in <setjmp.h>

questions.question: What are the return values of sigsetjmp and when are they returned?
keyPoints: Returns 0 when called directly (initial call)
Returns val (passed to siglongjmp) when returning via siglongjmp
If val is 0, sigsetjmp returns 1 (to distinguish from initial call)
This return mechanism distinguishes between initial call and jump-back

questions.question: Why should volatile variables be used with siglongjmp?
keyPoints: siglongjmp bypasses normal function call/return flow
Compiler optimizations may assume variable values are unchanged
volatile prevents compiler from optimizing away variable reads/writes
Without volatile, values may appear incorrect after siglongjmp returns

questions.question: Write a small C code snippet demonstrating sigsetjmp/siglongjmp usage.
keyPoints: Include <setjmp.h> and <signal.h>
Declare sigjmp_buf jump_buffer
Call sigsetjmp(jump_buffer, 1) and check return value
In signal handler, call siglongjmp with non-zero value
Use volatile for variables that must persist

questions.question: What are the potential issues if setjmp/longjmp is used instead of sigsetjmp/siglongjmp in signal handling?
keyPoints: Signal mask remains blocked after returning from signal handler
Signals may be delivered to wrong handlers
No proper restoration of signal handling state
Can lead to deadlocks or unhandled signals in programs

questions.question: Explain in detail how sigsetjmp and siglongjmp work together for error recovery in signal handlers with a complete example.
keyPoints: sigsetjmp saves execution context and signal mask when called with savemask=1
On initial call returns 0, on jump-back returns the value from siglongjmp
Signal handler installs using sigaction (preferred) or signal
When signal occurs, handler calls siglongjmp to restore saved context
This allows program to jump out of handler to a known recovery point
Signal mask is properly restored, preventing blocked signals issue
Complete example showing setup, handler, and recovery code
Use volatile for critical variables

questions.question: Compare and contrast setjmp/longjmp with sigsetjmp/siglongjmp under the following headings: (a) Buffer type (b) Signal mask handling (c) Usage in signal handlers (d) Header file
keyPoints: (a) setjmp uses jmp_buf, sigsetjmp uses sigjmp_buf - larger structure
(b) setjmp cannot save/restore signal mask, sigsetjmp can via savemask parameter
(c) setjmp/longjmp may leave signals blocked, sigsetjmp/siglongjmp properly restores mask
(d) Both declared in <setjmp.h>
sigsetjmp preferred for any program dealing with signals
sigsetjmp adds overhead only when savemask is non-zero

questions.question: Design a signal-safe error recovery mechanism using sigsetjmp/siglongjmp for a program that performs critical file operations. Explain each component.
keyPoints: Define sigjmp_buf for jump buffer
Use sigsetjmp at beginning of critical section with savemask=1
Return value check distinguishes normal execution from recovery
Signal handler (installed with sigaction) calls siglongjmp on error
volatile sig_atomic_t for flags
volatile for any variables used across jump
Proper cleanup in recovery path
Example showing file open, operation, error signal, recovery

questions.question: What are the constraints and undefined behaviors to be aware of when using siglongjmp?
keyPoints: Cannot jump to a context that no longer exists (function has returned) - undefined behavior
Variables with automatic storage that have changed may have indeterminate values
Should not jump into or out of setjmp/longjmp across different contexts
volatile should be used for variables that must retain values
If val passed is 0, sigsetjmp returns 1 instead
Signal mask restoration may affect pending signals
Should not be used recursively - nested usage has implementation limits
